title: Global Packaging Market Set to Reach USD 1.75 Trillion by 2035 Driven by Sustainability and Smart Innovation
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global packaging market is projected to grow from USD 1.32 trillion in 2026 to USD 1.75 trillion by 2035 at a CAGR of 3.16%, driven by rising demand for sustainable, biodegradable, and reusable packaging solutions. Asia-Pacific leads the market while North America is expected to expand notably. Key growth drivers include e-commerce expansion, healthcare sector demands, and innovations in sustainable materials, though rising raw material costs and supply chain disruptions pose challenges.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major North American packaging company positioned to benefit from market growth driven by sustainability trends and North America's expected expansion during 2026-2035 forecast period.
Major rigid packaging manufacturer benefiting from growing demand for rigid packaging segment, which held dominant market share in 2025.
European packaging company positioned to capitalize on paper and paperboard segment dominance and sustainability-driven market growth in Europe.

title: Pharmaceutical Packaging Market to Exceed $219B by 2031 as Sustainability & Injectable Drug Packaging Reshape Industry Dynamics, Says Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global pharmaceutical packaging market is projected to grow from USD 154.78 billion in 2025 to USD 219.08 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 5.94%. Growth is driven by increasing demand for injectable therapies, sustainable packaging solutions, and stricter regulatory traceability requirements. Key trends include the shift toward recyclable materials, adoption of prefilled syringes, and advanced barrier systems.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major company in the pharmaceutical packaging market, positioned to benefit from growing demand for sustainable and innovative packaging solutions across the expanding market.
Major competitor in glass and ready-to-use container formats, well-positioned to capitalize on the shift toward advanced polymer and glass solutions for injectable therapies.
Specializes in prefilled syringes and injectable packaging solutions, directly benefiting from the expanding biologics pipeline and growing demand for convenient self-administration formats.
Involved in advanced packaging technologies and closures, positioned to benefit from innovation in smart packaging and RFID-enabled verification systems.
Major player in prefilled syringes and injectable drug delivery systems, well-positioned to capture growth from the expanding biologics and injectable therapies pipeline.
Major player in secondary packaging and sustainable materials, well-positioned to capitalize on the industry shift toward recyclable and low-carbon packaging solutions.
Key player in pharmaceutical packaging with exposure to growing injectable drug packaging segment and advanced barrier systems innovation.

title: Unlocking Cost-Efficiency in Lightweight Industrial Corrugated Packaging
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global lightweight industrial corrugated packaging market is expected to grow from $40 billion in 2025 to $75.09 billion by 2035, driven by e-commerce expansion, sustainability demands, and manufacturing innovations. Asia Pacific leads the market, with China and North America experiencing significant growth. Key trends include micro-flute adoption, digital printing, and the shift toward recyclable alternatives to plastic.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Committed over $43 million to expand paper-based aseptic carton facilities, showing significant capital investment and strategic focus on sustainable, lightweight solutions.
Manufactures high-performance micro-flute containers addressing market demand for reduced material usage without sacrificing strength, well-positioned for market growth.
Supplies high-strength, lightweight containerboard grades designed to lower shipping costs and environmental impact, aligned with market growth drivers.
Offers 'Fit-to-Size' corrugated solutions and lightweighted papers eliminating excess material, addressing key market trends in weight optimization.
Introduced Performa Lumi lightweight folding boxboard in January 2026 using FiberLight technology, demonstrating recent innovation and commitment to sustainable packaging.

title: Maximizing Profitability in the Rigid Paper Containers Sector Trends and Investments Shaping the Future
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global rigid paper containers market is projected to grow from USD 83.9 billion in 2025 to USD 119.5 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 3.6%. Major industry players are making significant investments through mergers and acquisitions, while key trends include sustainable packaging, smart packaging integration, and growth in food delivery segments. North America leads the market, with Asia-Pacific emerging as the fastest-growing region.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Completed a $12.7 billion merger creating one of the largest global packaging entities with focus on high-volume corrugated boxes and rigid paperboard containers. Recently launched innovative Bag-in-Box Powergrip solution to replace plastic, demonstrating commitment to sustainable innovation.
Acquired DS Smith for $7.2 billion, significantly expanding footprint in rigid paper formats and European corrugated market, positioning the company for stronger market presence and growth.
Introduced innovative Boardio rigid paperboard canister in December 2025 that cuts plastic usage by over 90%, demonstrating technological innovation and alignment with sustainability trends.
Investing over $100 million through 2025 to establish new manufacturing facilities for aseptic rigid cartons, demonstrating confidence in market demand and commitment to capacity expansion.
Provides sustainable solid fiberboard and specialized paperboard solutions, well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand for eco-friendly packaging materials.

title: U.S. Beer Packaging Market Projected Growth from USD 6.30 Billion in 2025 to USD 9.06 Billion by 2035
author: Towards Packaging
description: The U.S. beer packaging market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 3.7% from USD 6.30 billion in 2025 to USD 9.06 billion by 2035. Growth is driven by craft beer demand, premiumization, e-commerce expansion, and sustainability trends. Glass bottles currently dominate, while aluminum cans are expected to grow fastest. Major industry players are investing heavily in sustainable, recyclable packaging solutions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Expanding Heritage glass bottle collection with lighter, shorter designs to improve transport efficiency and reduce carbon footprint, demonstrating commitment to sustainability and innovation.
Invested over $600 million in Michigan facility for sustainable fiber-based multipack carriers, showing significant capital commitment to meet growing beverage packaging demand.
$20 billion merger created a massive entity focused on scaling sustainable corrugated and paper packaging solutions for large-scale beer distribution.
Specializes in aluminum beverage cans and high-performance metal ends/closures, positioned to benefit from fastest-growing metal segment in the market.
Supplies rigid PET bottles with barrier technologies and recyclable flexible films, well-positioned in growing sustainable packaging segment.
Executing $750 million investment initiative to modernize manufacturing and improve energy efficiency, positioning itself for market growth in beer bottle production.
Continuing facility upgrades to meet high consumer demand for infinitely recyclable aluminum cans, which currently dominate the beer market.
